description: branch of Freedom Democratic party during 1960s Civil Rights Movement
description_wiki: The Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party (MFDP), also referred to simply as the Freedom Democratic Party, was an American political party that existed in the state of Mississippi from 1964 to 1968 during the Civil Rights Movement.

description: series of presidential nominating conventions held every four years since 1832 by the United States Democratic Party

description: American civil rights activist (1935–2021)
description_wiki: Robert Parris Moses (January 23, 1935 – July 25, 2021) was an American educator and civil rights activist known for his work as a leader of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) on voter education and registration in Mississippi during the Civil Rights Movement, and his co-founding of the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party.

aliases: Democratic Party of the United States

description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.

description_wiki: Chattanooga ( CHAT-ə-NOO-gə) is a city in Hamilton County, Tennessee, United States, and its county seat.
